message:
feat(pt-core): Enhance session management and supervision

- Updated beads issue tracker state
- Improved .gitignore patterns
- Enhanced session/mod.rs with better session handling
- Cleaned up signature_cli.rs
- Improved supervision pattern_learning module
- Enhanced TUI config editor widget
- Updated toon integration documentation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"process_triage-bra","title":"EPIC: Session Bundles and HTML Reports","description":"## Overview
Implement shareable session bundles (.ptb) and premium single-file HTML reports for sharing and postmortems.

## Core Requirements (from Plan Section 3.6)

### Bundle Format (.ptb)
A single portable file containing:
- **Container**: Prefer ZIP for cross-platform + in-browser parsing; allow tar.zst for power users
- **manifest.json**: Schema versions, tool versions/capabilities, host fingerprint, redaction policy version, checksums
- **plan.json**: Candidates + recommended actions + gates
- **telemetry/**: Parquet partitions or reduced aggregates (profile-dependent)
- **raw/**: Optional; capped and redacted
- **report.html**: Single static file with CDN-loaded libs

### Export Profiles
- **--profile minimal**: Plan + summary only (max safe, tiny)
- **--profile safe**: Includes derived features/inference but aggressively redacts raw strings/paths/endpoints
- **--profile forensic**: Includes more raw data; optionally support --encrypt for transport

### HTML Report (Single File, CDN-Loaded)

**Requirements**:
- Single `report.html` that works when opened directly (file://)
- Embed plan + derived summaries directly in HTML (avoid relying on local Parquet)
- Optional deep mode: dropzone to load .ptb via file picker, unpack in-memory

**Visual Polish**:
- Overview dashboard
- Sortable/searchable candidate table
- Per-process drilldown: timelines, evidence ledger, process tree, dependency impact
- Actions/outcomes with before/after diffs

**CDN-Loaded Library Stack** (example; exact choices can evolve):
- UI: Tailwind or PicoCSS + custom CSS for premium spacing/typography
- Tables: Tabulator (sorting/filtering/search, expandable rows)
- Charts: ECharts or Plotly (time series + distributions + sparklines)
- Graphs/trees: Mermaid (process tree + action DAG)
- Code/math: highlight.js + KaTeX/MathJax (for galaxy-brain tab)
- Optional: DuckDB-WASM for in-browser Parquet queries

**Security**:
- Pinned versions + SRI integrity hashes for all CDN assets

**Offline Mode**:
- `--embed-assets` to inline third-party assets when CDNs unavailable
- Default remains CDN-loaded

### Report Content

**Overview Dashboard**:
- Session summary metrics
- Resource recovery achieved
- Risk assessment

**Candidate Table**:
- Sortable by score, PID, runtime, memory
- Searchable by command, user
- Expandable rows for detail

**Per-Process Drilldown**:
- Evidence ledger with Bayes factors
- Process tree visualization
- Timeline of observations
- Dependency impact analysis

**Actions & Outcomes**:
- Before/after diff
- Success/failure status per action
- Recovery hints for failures

**Galaxy-Brain Tab**:
- Math ledger with equations + numbers
- Respects redaction policies

### CLI Commands
```
pt agent export --session <id> --profile <minimal|safe|forensic> --out session.ptb
pt agent report --session <id> --out report.html
pt agent report --session <id> --embed-assets --out report-offline.html
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] .ptb bundle format implemented (ZIP container)
- [ ] All export profiles work (minimal, safe, forensic)
- [ ] manifest.json with proper checksums
- [ ] HTML report generates as single file
- [ ] Report works when opened via file://
- [ ] CDN assets have pinned versions and SRI
- [ ] Sortable/searchable candidate table
- [ ] Per-process drilldown works
- [ ] Galaxy-brain tab included
- [ ] --embed-assets produces offline-capable report
- [ ] Dropzone for .ptb loading works

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Session model, Telemetry, Inference
- Blocks: None (shareable artifact)

## Technical Notes
- ZIP creation using standard Rust libraries
- HTML template should be embedded in binary
- CDN asset versions pinned in code
- SRI hashes computed at build time","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:52:30.070432563Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T07:05:53.475552986Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T07:05:53.475552986Z","close_reason":"All children complete (k4yc.3 bundle writer, k4yc.5 HTML report, k4yc.4 encryption)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-bra","depends_on_id":"process_triage-k4yc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:05:22.696309994Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"process_triage-brh7","title":"Implement VOI (Value of Information) computation","description":"## Overview
Implement **Value of Information (VOI)** for deciding whether to gather more evidence (probe / wait / deep scan) or act now.

This is the core mechanism behind the plan’s “maximal instrumentation, budgeted execution” principle: measure more **only** when it is expected to materially reduce decision risk.

## Plan References
- Plan §5.3 (VOI)
- Plan §5.9 (budgeted instrumentation / Whittle-style scheduling)
- Plan §5.10 (active sensing action selection)

## Mathematical Foundation
VOI compares acting now vs acting after one more measurement (or a wait window):

- Let `b` be the current belief/posterior over states.
- Let `a*` be the minimum expected-loss action under `b`.

```
VOI(m) = E_y[ min_a E[L(a,S) | b ⊕ (m,y)] ]  -  min_a E[L(a,S) | b ]  -  cost(m)

Act now if: min_m VOI(m) ≥ 0
Probe with m* if: VOI(m*) < 0  (i.e., probing reduces expected loss enough to pay its cost)
```

Notes:
- `cost(m)` includes wall-clock time, overhead budget, intrusiveness, and any added risk from the probe itself.
- For conjugate families, `E_y[...]` can often be computed via prior predictives or efficient approximations.

## Decision Outputs
VOI drives:
1) whether to deepen instrumentation for a PID,
2) which probe to run next,
3) “what would change your mind” explanations (flip conditions are often high-VOI signals).

## Probe Set (Examples)
The system must support a policy-defined probe catalog, e.g.:
- `wait_Δt`: free-ish, slow
- `deep_scan`: moderate cost, broad signal
- `stack_sample` / `perf_sample`: higher cost, high specificity
- `strace`/`sysdig`: high cost, intrusive
- `net_snapshot`: moderate cost

## Active Sensing Selection (Plan §5.10)
Select probe `m` to maximize expected risk reduction per cost, e.g. entropy reduction or expected loss reduction:

```
argmax_m  E_y[ H(b) - H(b ⊕ (m,y)) ] / cost(m)
```

For tractability, allow approximations:
- Fisher information / local Laplace approximations for exponential families
- Monte Carlo with capped samples in shadow-mode/offline validation only

## Whittle / Bandit Scheduling (Plan §5.9)
When overhead is limited and many PIDs compete for probes:
- treat each PID as an arm
- compute a per-PID index approximating marginal value of allocating one more probe budget unit
- schedule probes for the highest-index PIDs first

## Output Schema (Agent + Human)
VOI results must be surfaced in:
- the evidence ledger (why we probed / didn’t probe)
- agent JSON output
- the “what if” explainer

Example structure:
```json
{
  "voi_analysis": {
    "current_expected_loss": {"keep": 28.2, "pause": 15.1, "kill": 6.4},
    "probes": [
      {"probe": "wait_15min", "voi": -2.1, "cost": 0.5, "ratio": 4.2},
      {"probe": "deep_scan", "voi": -3.5, "cost": 1.0, "ratio": 3.5}
    ],
    "best_probe": "wait_15min",
    "act_now": false
  }
}
```

Interpretation:
- negative `voi` means probing reduces expected loss enough to justify cost.

## Deliverables
- Rust module: `decision/voi.rs`
- Per-probe cost model (policy-configurable)
- VOI computation utilities (expected-loss delta)
- Integration points:
  - probe scheduler
  - “what would change your mind” explainer
  - time-to-decision bound logic (`T_max`)
- Unit tests + documentation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] VOI computation implemented for a baseline probe catalog.
- [ ] Cost model is policy-configurable and auditable in logs/telemetry.
- [ ] Probe selection behaves sensibly on synthetic fixtures (probes chosen when they change posteriors materially).
- [ ] Output includes a clear VOI breakdown (per probe) for explainability.

## Test Plan
- Unit: VOI math on small synthetic posteriors with closed-form expected losses.
- Unit: cost model evaluation (time/overhead/intrusiveness).
- Integration: probe scheduling picks the highest-VOI candidate set under a fixed budget.
- E2E: `pt agent explain --what-if` includes VOI-backed flip conditions.

 {"id":"process_triage-bssn","title":"Implement causal intervention modeling (do-calculus)","description":"## Section 4.29 - Causal Intervention / do-Calculus

**Purpose**: Distinguish correlation from causation in process analysis. 'Killing parent causes children to orphan' vs 'orphaned children correlate with dead parents' require causal reasoning.

**Mathematical Background**:
- do-operator: P(Y|do(X=x)) ≠ P(Y|X=x) in general
- Adjustment formula: P(Y|do(X)) = Σ_z P(Y|X,Z)P(Z) if Z is valid adjustment set
- Backdoor criterion: Z blocks all backdoor paths from X to Y
- Front-door criterion: Z is caused by X and causes Y, with no confounders
- Structural causal model (SCM): X_i = f_i(PA_i, U_i) with exogenous noise U

**Implementation Requirements**:
1. `causal_effect(graph, treatment, outcome, adjustment_set)` - P(Y|do(X))
2. `identify_adjustment_set(graph, treatment, outcome)` - Find valid Z
3. `counterfactual(scm, observation, intervention)` - What if we had done X=x'?
4. `mediation_analysis(graph, treatment, mediator, outcome)` - Direct/indirect effects

**Why This Matters for pt**:
'If we kill this process, will dependent processes fail?' is causal, not correlational. do-calculus lets us predict intervention effects from observational data.

**Integration Points**:
- Dependency analysis (Section 4.19)
- Action planning (Section 6.1)
- Safety gates (Section 5.6)

**Test Requirements**:
- Verify adjustment formula matches experimental data
- Verify backdoor criterion identification
- Test counterfactual predictions on synthetic SCMs","notes":"Duplicate/out-of-tree bead. Canonical implementation: process_triage-p15.4.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:49:51.474129130Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T10:22:42.334886554Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T10:22:42.334886554Z","close_reason":"duplicate (canonical: process_triage-p15.4)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-bssn","depends_on_id":"process_triage-2z3g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:57:16.317465246Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"process_triage-bwn","title":"EPIC: Agent/Robot CLI Parity Layer","description":"## Overview
Implement the **agent/robot CLI contract** from the Alien Artifact plan (Plan §3.5) as a first-class, stable interface.

This epic exists because:
- AI agents need a **non-interactive**, schema-stable interface (no TUI assumptions).
- Human UX can evolve, but agent contracts must remain backwards compatible.
- The plan explicitly calls out agent primitives: plan/explain/apply/status/export/inbox/watch/learning/fleet/snapshot/capabilities/verify/diff.

## Plan References
- Plan §3.5 (Agent/Robot CLI Contract; “No TUI”)
- Plan §3.5.1 (Session continuity for agent workflows)
- Plan §7.4 / §7.7 (robot mode and sharing/reporting surfaces)

## Core Contract Principles
- **No prompts** in agent mode (stdin may be closed; must never hang).
- **Stable schemas** with explicit versioning; backward compatible changes only.
- **Token-efficient outputs** (support summaries + selective expansion).
- **Deterministic exit codes** and machine-readable error codes.
- **Idempotent + resumable workflows** (session ids; strict identity revalidation).

## Command Surface (Canonical)
This epic implements (and keeps stable):
1) `pt agent plan` (create/compute)
2) `pt agent explain` (drill-down)
3) `pt agent apply` (execute, no UI)
4) `pt agent sessions` (status / continuity)
5) `pt agent export` / `pt agent report`
6) `pt agent inbox` (daemon-driven plans)
7) `pt agent watch` (background monitoring)
8) `pt agent list-priors` / `import-priors` / `export-priors` (learning mgmt)
9) `pt agent fleet ...` (multi-host; implemented under fleet epic)
10) `pt agent snapshot`
11) `pt agent capabilities`
12) `pt agent verify`
13) `pt agent diff`

## Output Model
Agent outputs must include, in schema-stable form:
- session_id + artifact pointers
- candidate identity tuple `(pid,start_id,uid,boot_id,pgid,sid,...)`
- posterior over states + key evidence summaries
- expected loss table + chosen action
- safety gate evaluations (why blocked / allowed)
- progress events (JSONL stream)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All listed agent commands exist with stable schemas + exit codes.
- [ ] Commands never prompt or hang in non-interactive contexts.
- [ ] Apply/verify enforce strict identity revalidation and fail closed on mismatch.
- [ ] Tests validate contract shapes and “stdin closed” behavior.
","notes":"## API Harmonization Notes (2026-01-22)

### Consolidation Decisions

1. **show/status → sessions**: The `agent show` and `agent status` commands from the spec
   have been consolidated into `agent sessions`. Use:
   - `pt agent sessions` - List all sessions
   - `pt agent sessions --session <id>` - Show full session details
   - The `--status` flag is deprecated in favor of `--session`

2. **export alias**: `pt agent export` added as alias for `bundle create` for ergonomics.

3. **Naming unification**: `--min-posterior` is now the canonical name, `--threshold` is an alias.

### Child Epic

 {"id":"process_triage-bwn.1","title":"Implement genealogy narrative generation for agent explain","description":"## Context
Plan Phase 15 (Enhanced UX for Agents): genealogy narrative generation.

## Problem
When an agent/human sees a suspicious process, they often need “where did this come from?” A raw PPID chain is not enough; we want a readable story:
- parent shell → test runner → worker → orphaned child
- supervisor → respawn loop

## Approach
- Build ancestry chain from PPID forest + collected context:
  - parent command, cwd, tty, user/session, supervisor signals
- Annotate roles along the chain (worker, server, test runner, user shell, supervisor, etc.).
- Generate outputs:
  - structured JSON `genealogy` field (list of nodes with annotations)
  - human-readable `genealogy_narrative` string (brief + verbose modes)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Produces stable ancestry chains for fixture trees.
- [ ] Role annotations are deterministic and documented.
- [ ] Narrative text is concise by default and expandable via flag.

## Test Plan
- Unit tests: role annotation rules.
- Golden tests: fixture PPID trees → narrative output.

 {"id":"process_triage-bwn.2","title":"Implement blast radius analysis (children/ports/files)","description":"## Context
Plan Phase 15 (Enhanced UX for Agents): blast radius visualization/analysis.

## Problem
Killing a process can have downstream impact. We need to estimate and present blast radius so agents/humans can make safer decisions.

## Scope
- Enumerate child processes (PPID subtree)
- Detect dependent ports (lsof/ss/netstat where available)
- Detect open files, especially write handles (locks, WAL/journals)
- Compute a cumulative risk score and list of “why risky” factors

## Output
- Structured JSON: `blast_radius` with counts + key dependencies
- Human summary: “Kills 12 children; holds write lock on X; serves port 3000.”

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correctly identifies children and open ports on fixture processes.
- [ ] Surfaces open write FDs and escalates risk accordingly.
- [ ] Provides stable structured output for agents.

## Test Plan
- Integration tests: spawn a process tree and verify child enumeration.
- Integration tests: bind a port and verify detection.
- E2E: simulate file locks and verify risk flags.

 {"id":"process_triage-bwn.3","title":"Implement what-if / flip-conditions explainer","description":"## Context
Plan Phase 15: “what would change your mind” support.

## Problem
Users/agents should understand not only why pt recommends an action, but what evidence would reverse it. This builds trust and guides further investigation (“collect X to resolve uncertainty”).

## Approach
- From the posterior + evidence ledger, compute flip conditions:
  - which evidence terms dominate
  - what delta in a key feature would reduce P(abandoned) below threshold
- Output:
  - top-K flip scenarios with estimated delta_p and required evidence change
  - optionally recommended additional probes (links to VOI/probe selection)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Produces deterministic flip-condition rankings on fixture ledgers.
- [ ] Output is explainable: each flip cites the exact ledger terms involved.
- [ ] Integrates into agent explain output behind a flag.

## Test Plan
- Unit tests: flip-condition computation on synthetic ledgers.
- Golden tests: fixture → ranked flip scenarios.

 {"id":"process_triage-qqzr","title":"Fix test compilation errors from pt-config refactoring","description":"## Problem
Test compilation is broken with ~70+ errors after pt-config refactoring.

## Root Cause
1. Tests import non-existent `Classes` type (should be `ClassPriors`)
2. Tests use `ClassPriors` where they mean `ClassParams` 
3. `BetaParams` struct literals missing `comment` field
4. `GammaParams` struct literals missing `comment` field

## Affected Files
- crates/pt-core/tests/what_if_explanations.rs (13 BetaParams)
- crates/pt-core/tests/schema_validation.rs (3 BetaParams)
- crates/pt-core/src/decision/expected_loss.rs (12 BetaParams)
- crates/pt-core/src/decision/causal_interventions.rs (19 BetaParams)
- crates/pt-core/src/inference/posterior.rs (5 BetaParams)
- crates/pt-core/src/inference/beta_stacy.rs (4 BetaParams)
- Plus supervision/signature.rs tests

## Fix Approach
1. Replace `BetaParams { alpha: X, beta: Y }` with `BetaParams::new(X, Y)`
2. Fix import statements to use correct type names
3. Update `ClassPriors` usages to `ClassParams` where appropriate

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` compiles successfully
- [ ] All tests pass or have known failures","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SapphireSpring","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:00:20.715362147Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:29:13.263020730Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T18:29:13.263020730Z","close_reason":"Closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"process_triage-qr2","title":"Implement pt agent tail command","description":"## Overview
Implement the `pt agent tail` command for streaming progress events during long-running operations.

## From Plan Section 3.5

### Command Purpose
Stream JSONL progress events so agents can monitor ongoing operations. Essential for showing staged progress (scan → deep scan → infer → decide).

### Usage
```
pt agent tail --session <id>
pt agent tail --follow        # Wait for new events
```

### Event Types (JSONL Stream)
```jsonl
{'event': 'phase_start', 'phase': 'quick_scan', 'timestamp': '...'}
{'event': 'progress', 'phase': 'quick_scan', 'progress': 0.5, 'message': '206/412 processes scanned'}
{'event': 'phase_complete', 'phase': 'quick_scan', 'duration_ms': 1200, 'result': {'candidates': 15}}
{'event': 'phase_start', 'phase': 'deep_scan', 'timestamp': '...'}
{'event': 'progress', 'phase': 'deep_scan', 'progress': 0.3, 'message': 'Scanning PID 1234...'}
{'event': 'tool_run', 'tool': 'lsof', 'pid': 1234, 'duration_ms': 50}
{'event': 'phase_complete', 'phase': 'deep_scan', 'duration_ms': 5000}
{'event': 'phase_start', 'phase': 'inference', 'timestamp': '...'}
{'event': 'phase_complete', 'phase': 'inference', 'duration_ms': 200}
{'event': 'plan_ready', 'candidates': 7, 'recommended_kills': 3}
{'event': 'action_start', 'action': 'kill', 'target': {'pid': 1234}}
{'event': 'action_complete', 'action': 'kill', 'target': {'pid': 1234}, 'result': 'success'}
{'event': 'session_complete', 'outcomes': {...}}
```

### Integration with TUI
- Same events drive TUI progress display
- Shows staged progress in system bar
- Inline sparklines use telemetry from events

### Follow Mode
- --follow waits for new events
- Uses file watching or named pipe
- Exits when session completes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] JSONL events for all phases
- [ ] Progress percentage and messages
- [ ] Tool run events with timing
- [ ] Action events with outcomes
- [ ] --follow mode works
- [ ] Events persisted to session artifacts

## Dependencies
- Session model
- Phase 3 (evidence collection for tool events)

## Technical Notes
- Events written to session/events.jsonl
- Use file watching for --follow
- Events should be append-only","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Dicklesworthstone","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:54:27.090147535Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T08:39:54.885165224Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T08:39:54.885168170Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-qr2","depends_on_id":"process_triage-bwn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:12:15.372814596Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"process_triage-qr2","depends_on_id":"process_triage-f5o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T12:47:32.886022221Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"process_triage-qr2","depends_on_id":"process_triage-t6lf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:09:04.782590118Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":59,"issue_id":"process_triage-qr2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed by CloudyFalcon; investigating session event persistence and tail implementation plan.","created_at":"2026-01-16T08:28:26Z"},{"id":60,"issue_id":"process_triage-qr2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented agent tail + session log emitter (events/session.jsonl under logs). Added SessionEmitter/FanoutEmitter and tail command. cargo test -p pt-core events::tests::test_session_emitter_attaches_session_id failed due to unrelated untracked signature_fast_path.rs compile errors (missing BetaParams import).","created_at":"2026-01-16T08:34:10Z"},{"id":61,"issue_id":"process_triage-qr2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added inference/decision progress events in agent plan (INFERENCE_STARTED/PROGRESS/COMPLETE, DECISION_STARTED/COMPLETE) so tail shows stage progression.","created_at":"2026-01-16T08:38:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"process_triage-r2of","title":"Implement pt agent import-priors command","description":"**Purpose**: Implement the `pt agent import-priors` command for bootstrapping machines from external priors.

**Plan Document Reference**: Section 3.5.1 (8. Learning)

**CLI Surface**:
```
pt agent import-priors --from priors.json [OPTIONS]
```

**Required Options**:
- `--from <path>` - Input priors file
- `--merge` - Merge with existing priors (weighted average)
- `--replace` - Replace existing priors entirely
- `--host-profile <name>` - Apply to specific host profile

**Merge Strategies**:
- `--merge` performs weighted combination of existing and imported priors
- Weights can be based on observation counts (more data = higher weight)
- `--replace` overwrites completely (useful for fleet standardization)

**Validation**:
- Verify schema compatibility
- Warn if host profile mismatch (e.g., importing server priors to devbox)
- Report what changed after import

**Safety**:
- Creates backup of existing priors before modification
- Provides dry-run mode to preview changes
- Logs import operation for audit trail

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Implements the described scope with deterministic behavior.
- [ ] Emits structured logs/telemetry for key decisions and failures (as applicable).
- [ ] Includes unit/integration/E2E coverage for the highest-risk paths.

### Description
Implement comprehensive JSON output format for agent consumption with all necessary data.

### Requirements
1. **JSON Output Schema**
   ```json
   {
     "pt_version": "2.1.0",
     "timestamp": "2024-01-15T14:30:00Z",
     "host": {
       "hostname": "dev-workstation-01",
       "cores": 64,
       "memory_total_gb": 512,
       "memory_used_gb": 280,
       "load_avg": [5.17, 4.89, 10.23]
     },
     "scan": {
       "total_processes": 412,
       "candidates_found": 23,
       "scan_duration_ms": 1250
     },
     "candidates": [
       {
         "pid": 12345,
         "ppid": 1000,
         "user": "developer",
         "command": "bun test --watch tests/",
         "command_normalized": "bun test --watch .*",
         "type": "test_runner",
         "age_seconds": 950400,
         "age_human": "11d 0h 0m",
         "memory_mb": 2048,
         "cpu_percent": 0.0,
         "score": 85,
         "confidence": 0.92,
         "recommendation": "KILL",
         "evidence": [
           {"factor": "process_type_prior", "contribution": 25, "detail": "test_runner"},
           {"factor": "age_vs_expected", "contribution": 30, "detail": "11x expected lifetime"},
           {"factor": "orphaned", "contribution": 20, "detail": "PPID=1"},
           {"factor": "zero_cpu", "contribution": 15, "detail": "0% for 4h"},
           {"factor": "memory", "contribution": -5, "detail": "low usage"}
         ],
         "genealogy": {
           "parent": {"pid": 1000, "command": "bash"},
           "children": [],
           "depth": 2
         },
         "blast_radius": {
           "descendants": 0,
           "total_memory_mb": 2048,
           "connections": 0
         }
       }
     ],
     "recommendations": {
       "kill_set": [12345, 23456],
       "review_set": [34567],
       "spare_set": [45678],
       "expected_memory_freed_gb": 8.2,
       "fleet_fdr": 0.03
     }
   }
   ```

2. **Output Modes**
   ```bash
   # Full JSON output
   pt agent snapshot --format=json
   
   # Minimal JSON (just PIDs and scores)
   pt agent snapshot --format=json --minimal
   
   # Pretty-printed JSON
   pt agent snapshot --format=json --pretty
   ```

3. **Streaming Output**
   - For long-running deep scans, emit NDJSON (one object per line)
   - Progress updates as separate JSON objects
   - Final summary as last object

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] JSON output validates against schema
- [ ] All evidence is included with contributions
- [ ] Streaming mode works for deep scans
- [ ] Output is parseable by jq","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:06:45.976440250Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T09:06:45.976440250Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-raze","depends_on_id":"process_triage-bwn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:12:15.414121843Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"process_triage-raze","depends_on_id":"process_triage-s8s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:09:00.838284794Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"process_triage-ref7","title":"Implement fleet mode tests","description":"## Test Requirements: Fleet Mode (Section 11.11)

### Unit Tests
1. **Host registration**: Test host joining fleet with capabilities
2. **Host heartbeat**: Test heartbeat protocol and failure detection
3. **Message serialization**: Test JSON message format for fleet protocol
4. **FDR pooling**: Test pooled e-value computation across hosts

### Protocol Tests
1. **Leader election**: Test leader election on coordinator failure
2. **Split-brain prevention**: Test quorum requirements (>50% hosts)
3. **Message ordering**: Test causal ordering of fleet messages
4. **Idempotent operations**: Test retry safety for all operations

### Test Scenarios
```
SCENARIO: single_host_degradation
  Setup: 3 hosts, 1 becomes unreachable
  Expected: Fleet continues with 2 hosts, FDR adjusted

SCENARIO: coordinator_failure
  Setup: Coordinator host crashes
  Expected: New leader elected within 30s

SCENARIO: network_partition
  Setup: 2+2 host partition
  Expected: Larger partition continues, smaller pauses

SCENARIO: rolling_restart
  Setup: Hosts restart one at a time
  Expected: Fleet remains operational throughout

SCENARIO: cross_host_correlation
  Setup: Same process pattern on 5 hosts
  Expected: Pooled confidence higher than individual
```

### Integration Tests
1. **5-host fleet**: Test full fleet operations with 5 simulated hosts
2. **High-frequency updates**: Test 100 updates/second across fleet
3. **Large process lists**: Test 10000 processes across fleet
4. **Heterogeneous hosts**: Test hosts with different capabilities

### Mathematical Tests
1. **Pooled FDR**: Verify q = Σe_i / n formula
2. **Stratified FDR**: Test per-host α allocation
3. **Sequential FDR**: Test alpha-investing across time

### Performance Tests
- Fleet sync must complete in <1s for 10 hosts
- FDR computation must complete in <100ms for 10000 processes

### Logging Requirements
- Log all fleet protocol messages
- Log leader election events
- Log FDR pooling computations
- Log host join/leave events

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Implements the full scope described above (no silent omissions).
- [ ] Handles edge/error cases described above with deterministic behavior.
- [ ] Includes unit tests for the core logic and the highest-risk edge cases.
- [ ] Emits structured logs/telemetry for key decisions and failures (as applicable).

## Test Plan
- Unit: golden tests for core logic; edge cases and error paths.
- Integration: exercise real tool/system interactions where applicable (or mocked equivalents).
- E2E: cover at least one representative user/agent workflow when applicable.
- Logging: tests validate presence/shape of critical log/telemetry fields.

 {"id":"process_triage-rqef","title":"Implement PAC-Bayes generalization bounds","description":"## Section 4.33 - PAC-Bayes Generalization

**Purpose**: Provide probably approximately correct (PAC) bounds on generalization error of Bayesian classifiers. Ensure learned priors generalize to new processes, not just training data.

**Mathematical Background**:
- PAC-Bayes bound: E_ρ[L(h)] ≤ E_ρ[L̂(h)] + √[(KL(ρ||π) + log(2√n/δ))/(2n)]
  where ρ is posterior, π is prior, L̂ is empirical loss
- Data-dependent prior: π can depend on part of data (split trick)
- McAllester's bound: Uses KL directly, tightest for small KL
- Catoni's bound: PAC-Bayes with tighter constants
- Localized bounds: Condition on ρ being close to π

**Implementation Requirements**:
1. `pac_bayes_bound(posterior, prior, empirical_risk, n, delta)` - Compute bound
2. `optimal_posterior(prior, data, regularization)` - Minimize PAC-Bayes bound
3. `generalization_gap(training_error, pac_bound)` - Expected test error
4. `data_split_prior(data, split_ratio)` - Use part of data for prior

**Why This Matters for pt**:
Our classifier learns from user decisions. PAC-Bayes guarantees: 'With 95% probability, error rate on new processes ≤ 0.15'. This is stronger than just training accuracy.

**Integration Points**:
- Model validation (Section 4.14)
- Decision learning (Section 5.8)
- Confidence reporting (Section 7.1)

**Test Requirements**:
- Verify bound holds on held-out data
- Verify bound tightens with more training data
- Compare PAC-Bayes vs VC bounds on same classifier","notes":"Duplicate/out-of-tree bead. Canonical implementation: process_triage-72j.2.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:50:40.104506894Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T10:22:41.819582526Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T10:22:41.819582526Z","close_reason":"duplicate (canonical: process_triage-72j.2)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-rqef","depends_on_id":"process_triage-wb3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:57:21.079232768Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"process_triage-rqn","title":"Implement Beta distribution utilities","description":"## Task
Implement Beta distribution functions needed for posterior computation.

## Background
The Beta distribution is conjugate to Bernoulli/Binomial and appears throughout:
- Prior on Bernoulli success probability
- Posterior after observing successes/failures
- Posterior predictive for next observation

Required functions:
- beta_pdf(x, alpha, beta) - probability density
- beta_cdf(x, alpha, beta) - cumulative distribution
- beta_inv_cdf(p, alpha, beta) - quantile function
- beta_mean(alpha, beta) = alpha / (alpha + beta)
- beta_var(alpha, beta) = alpha*beta / ((alpha+beta)^2 * (alpha+beta+1))
- log_beta_pdf(x, alpha, beta) - log-domain for stability

## Implementation Notes
- Use log-beta function for numerical stability
- log_beta(a,b) = lgamma(a) + lgamma(b) - lgamma(a+b)
- Handle edge cases: x=0, x=1, alpha<1, beta<1
- Consider using Stirling approximation for large parameters

## Test Cases
- Beta(1,1) = Uniform(0,1)
- Beta(2,5): mean=2/7, mode=1/5
- Symmetry: Beta(a,b) at x = Beta(b,a) at 1-x
- Known quantiles from tables

## Deliverables
- Rust module: math/beta.rs
- Unit tests with comprehensive coverage
- Documentation with equations
- Benchmarks for performance verification

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `beta_mean`/`beta_var` match analytic values for common parameters.
- [ ] `beta_pdf` and `log_beta_pdf` agree (exp(log_pdf) ~= pdf) where numerically safe.
- [ ] `beta_cdf` is monotone and returns 0 at x=0 and 1 at x=1 (within tolerance).
- [ ] `beta_inv_cdf` inverts `beta_cdf` on a representative grid of p values.
- [ ] Edge cases (alpha/beta < 1, extreme alpha/beta) do not produce NaN/Inf for valid inputs.

## Test Plan
- Unit: golden values (mean/var/mode), symmetry checks, boundary x∈{0,1}.
- Property: inversion (`cdf(inv_cdf(p))≈p`), monotonicity.
- Perf: micro-benchmarks for cdf/inv_cdf to ensure feasibility at scale.
","notes":"Added Beta distribution utilities in crates/pt-math/src/math/beta.rs (pdf/log_pdf/cdf/inv_cdf/mean/var) using regularized incomplete beta with continued fraction; updated module exports in crates/pt-math/src/math/mod.rs and crates/pt-math/src/lib.rs. Tests cover mean/var, known pdf value, symmetry, log/pdf agreement, cdf identity, inversion, edge behavior. Ran: cargo test -p pt-math.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:23:03.702110629Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T14:25:18.893507652Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T14:25:18.893510888Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-rqn","depends_on_id":"process_triage-00b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:43:26.679979808Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"process_triage-rqn","depends_on_id":"process_triage-iau","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:10:03.283968302Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
